channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: This is NOT fixed, 2nd May
item.description:
This is an abandoned former cash point, formerly a phone box. GCC FixMyStreet has reported the issue as ‘fixed’. I attach a photo of the ‘fix’. This is not fixed by anyone’s standards. If GCC highways considers this fixed, it is time for a rethink. If it’s still a phone box, get BT to remove it. If it’s now street furniture owned by somebody else, get them to remove it. If GCC doesn’t have the power to remove it and can’t contact the owners, take enforcement action. You need to do better. Max Wilkinson MP

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70-72, High Street, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: For sale/let timber panel, 2nd May
item.description:
Obviously broken and left on verge
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A435, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can’t see the markings, 2nd May
item.description:
We can’t see the markings outside if the Mcdonald kingditch lane towards to big roundabout
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsditch Lane, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: Sunken Infill In Trench, 2nd May
item.description:
The tarmac in a trench dug right across the road has sunk, leaving a very sharp edged obstacle that smashes through car suspension when driven over. I notice this issue has been reported several times already and don’t understand how a hazard created by shoddy roadworks can be left un-repaired for months.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
